commentary: The liquidity hierarchy divides into three tiers. The North American leaders—Nasdaq 100 and NYSE—dominate with ILLIQ costs below 0.0056% per $1M traded and comparable volumes exceeding $222.4M, though Nasdaq's 0.7477% AR spread slightly lags NYSE's 0.6386%. The mid-pack comprises Tokyo, Euronext, SIX, Hong Kong, and Xetra, where ILLIQ ranges from 0.0294% to 0.0512% per $1M but volume fragmentation is pronounced—Tokyo generates $43.1M comparable volume despite middling spreads, while SIX's 0.5252% AR spread masks its thin $3.9M turnover, signaling illiquidity in smaller trades. Korea and London form the laggard tier; Korea Exchange stands out as the weakest link with 0.1465% per $1M ILLIQ and a 1.6361% AR spread, while London's 0.0779% per $1M ILLIQ and 0.7873% spread reflect structural challenges in post-Brexit execution. The divergence between Euronext's tight 0.6014% spread but relatively modest $13.9M volume warrants attention—spread efficiency masks underlying depth constraints for larger positions.
